name: CI/CD Dependency Hygiene
description: Strategies to ensure dependency parity between local development and CI/CD pipelines.

patterns.parity_check: Before pushing codes that introduce new imports (especially in tests or scripts), verify they are explicitly defined in requirements.txt or requirements-dev.txt.

best_practices: Run tests in a clean virtualenv periodically to catch missing implicit dependencies.
Categorize 'prototyping' libraries (like FastMCP) as dev-dependencies to keep production environments lean.
Always verify that all imported modules in tests/ integration suites are present in the CI runner's environment.
anti_patterns: Adding imports to integration tests that rely on local-only global installations.
Merging new server tools without checking if their core dependencies are in the primary requirements file.
Neglecting to update requirements-dev.txt for libraries used in notebooks or helper scripts.
